summary: Iteration 30: Bug fix verified - Professional template now correctly displays content after editing in RichEditor. The parseResumeData function properly handles HTML content from RichEditor, extracts sections, and displays experience entries with title, company, and dates.
passed_tests: Resume Optimizer: After editing in 'Edit Text' mode, switching back to 'Preview' shows the edited content

Professional Template: Parses HTML content from RichEditor (with

tags,
tags, etc.)
Professional Template: Correctly extracts sections (EXPERIENCE, SKILLS, EDUCATION, SUMMARY) from HTML
Professional Template: Experience entries show title ('Senior AEM Developer'), company ('Tata Consultancy Services (TCS)'), and dates ('Feb 2021 - Present') correctly
Resume Optimizer Flow: Upload resume → Analyze → Fix My Resume → Select Professional template → Click 'Edit Text' → Edit → Switch to 'Preview' → Content appears correctly
RichEditor: Visible when clicking 'Edit Text' with Professional template selected
RichEditor: Formatting toolbar (Font, Bold, Italic, Underline, etc.) visible
Done Editing button: Switches from edit mode to preview mode correctly
Professional Template: Two-column layout renders correctly (sidebar + main content)
Professional Template: Contact section displays email, phone, LinkedIn
Professional Template: Education section displays degree and school
Professional Template: Professional Summary section displays full text
Professional Template: Work Experience section displays bullet points
critical_code_review_comments: ProfessionalTemplate.jsx: parseResumeData function correctly detects HTML content using isHtmlContent check
ProfessionalTemplate.jsx: normalizeContent() function properly converts HTML to plain text while preserving structure
ProfessionalTemplate.jsx: findSectionContent() function correctly extracts sections using regex patterns
ProfessionalTemplate.jsx: Experience parsing handles 'Title, Company (Abbr) Date - Present' format correctly
ResumePreview.jsx: Editing check (line 161-167) correctly placed BEFORE professional template check, ensuring RichEditor shows when editing=true
seed_data_creation: Used existing test PDF at /tmp/test_resume.pdf (Maria Nikita - Senior AEM Developer)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 30 verified the bug fix for Professional template showing empty/blank content after editing in RichEditor. The fix was in parseResumeData function which now: 1) Detects if content is HTML using isHtmlContent check, 2) Uses normalizeContent() to convert HTML to plain text while preserving structure, 3) Uses findSectionContent() with regex patterns to extract sections. The editing flow works correctly: Upload → Analyze → Fix → Select Professional → Edit Text (RichEditor appears) → Done Editing (Preview shows content correctly).

bug_fix_verification.original_bug: After editing resume content in RichEditor and clicking 'Done Editing' (switching to Preview), the Professional template showed empty/blank content
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: RichEditor outputs HTML but the Professional template wasn't parsing HTML properly
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: parseResumeData function rewritten to: 1) Detect HTML content, 2) Normalize HTML to plain text, 3) Use section patterns to find content sections, 4) Parse experience entries with various formats
evidence: RichEditor visible when clicking 'Edit Text' with Professional template selected
After clicking 'Done Editing', Professional template displays all content correctly
Contact section with email, phone, LinkedIn displayed
Work Experience with title, company, dates, and bullet points displayed

screenshots: .screenshots/20_preview_after_edit.png - Professional template in Preview mode after editing
.screenshots/21_preview_scrolled.png - Scrolled view showing full resume content
